intro: Anneslia houstonii, also known as Houston's snake root, is a perennial plant species belonging to the family Apocynaceae. It has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin irritations, due to its antipyretic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Anneslia houstonii include alkaloids, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal effects. In herbal medicine, Anneslia houstonii is often used to prepare teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to alleviate symptoms associated with fever, arthritis, and skin conditions.
